title: Head Start * Native American Community - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Education > Head Start * Native American Community 8 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Migrants Native American Community Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF CONFEDERATED TRIBES OF GRAND RONDE HEAD START Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Tribal Head Start offers all-day and year-round preschool, providing a multicultural classroom experience. Head Start services promote school readiness by enhancing the social and cognitive development of children in a classroom setting and by providing educational, health, nutritional, social, and other services to children and families. Parental engagement is emphasized. Services for parents include goal setting and support, parenting classes and opportunities for parents to network. 9615 Grand Ronde Road Grand Ronde, OR 97347 Eligibility: Families with children ages 3-5. Families with incomes at or below 100% federal poverty level are eligible. Children in foster care, homeless children, and children from families receiving public assistance (Temporary Assistance for Needy Families or Supplemental Security Income) are eligible regardless of income. Families with higher incomes and specific needs may also be eligible, but families with higher needs are prioritized. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-2pm Email (503) 879-1434 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CONFEDERATED TRIBES OF SILETZ INDIANS HEAD START Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a developmentally appropriate and culturally relevant preschool experience rooted in Native American traditions. Head Start services foster school readiness by nurturing children’s social and cognitive development in a classroom environment. In addition, a comprehensive array of services, including educational, health, nutritional, and social support, benefits both children and families. Parental engagement is a key focus, with services that include goal-setting support, parenting classes, and opportunities for parents to connect and network. 201 SE Swan Avenue Siletz, OR 97380 Eligibility: Families with children ages 3-5. Preference for enrollment to children who are Native American. Hours: Monday-Thursday 8:30am-noon (541) 444-2532 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CONFEDERATED TRIBES OF THE UMATILLA INDIAN RESERVATION CAY-UMA-WA HEAD START HEAD START Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Head Start services promote school readiness by enhancing the social and cognitive development of children in a classroom setting and by providing educational, health, nutritional, social, and other services to children and families. Parental engagement is emphasized. Services for parents include goal setting and support, parenting classes, and opportunities for parents to network. 46250 Timine Way Pendleton, OR 97801 Eligibility: Families with children ages 3-5. Families with incomes at or below 100% federal poverty level (FPL) are eligible. Children in foster care, homeless children, and children from families receiving public assistance (Temporary Assistance for Needy Families, Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) or Supplemental Security Income) are eligible regardless of income. Families with higher incomes and specific needs may also be eligible, but families with higher needs are prioritized. Parental engagement is emphasized. Parental engagement is emphasized. Services for parents include goal setting and support, parenting classes, and opportunities for parents to network. 1257 Kot-Num Road Warm Springs, OR 97761 Eligibility: Warm Springs tribal families with children ages 3-5, reservation residents, and children of families employed on the reservation Hours: Monday-Friday 7:45am-5pm (541) 553-3242 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COQUILLE INDIAN TRIBE COQUILLE INDIAN TRIBE HEAD START PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Promotes school readiness by enhancing social, emotional, and cognitive development of children in a classroom setting. Provides educational, health, nutritional, and other services to children and families. Services for parents include goal setting and support, parenting classes, and opportunities for parents to network. Parental engagement emphasized. 1340 N Bayshore Drive Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: Tribal families living in Coos County, Kilkich residents, and non-tribal families with children aged 3-5 years. Tribal families are given first priority for enrollment followed by Native Americans of other Tribes and non-native children. Hours: September-May, Monday-Thursday 7:40am-3:45 pm (541) 888-7737 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FIND CHILDCARE OREGON FIND CHILD CARE OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps families locate child care through referrals and provides resources to help make informed decisions about child care needs. 211info is available to provide families referrals to support their child care search, or families can conduct their own search on the website. Website Eligibility: Parents/caregivers of children up to age 18 Hours: Monday-Friday 7am-11pm, Saturday-Sunday 8am-8pm Email 211 Visit Website More Details CONFEDERATED TRIBES OF GRAND RONDE HEAD START CONFEDERATED TRIBES OF SILETZ INDIANS HEAD START CONFEDERATED TRIBES OF THE UMATILLA INDIAN RESERVATION HEAD START CONFEDERATED TRIBES OF THE UMATILLA INDIAN RESERVATION HEAD START CONFEDERATED TRIBES OF WARM SPRINGS HEAD START COQUILLE INDIAN TRIBE COQUILLE INDIAN TRIBE HEAD START PROGRAM COQUILLE INDIAN TRIBE COQUILLE INDIAN TRIBE HEAD START PROGRAM
